name: B (Billion)
oneliner: The universal size marker. 7B = 7 billion parameters. Refers to total params unless combined with A (active).
explanation: Nearly universal. Some older models use lowercase (7b). When a model card lists a single number without total/active distinction, it's the total (and the model is dense).

name: T (Trillion)
oneliner: Trillions. Rare for params (Switch Transformer 1.6T). More common for training data ('trained on 15T tokens'). Context matters.
explanation: Ambiguous: '1T' in model size = trillion params. '1T tokens' = training data volume. Few public models exceed 1T params. Switch Transformer (1.6T) is the canonical example.

name: A (Active params)
expansion: Active parameters per token in MoE
oneliner: Active parameters per forward pass in MoE. Qwen2.5-A14B = 57B total, 14B active. Gemma 26B-A4B = 25.2B total, 3.8B active.
explanation: Crucial for understanding actual compute cost. All params must be in memory, but only A params compute per token. Introduced by Qwen MoE and Gemma teams.

name: E (Effective params)
oneliner: Google Gemma notation: effective capacity accounting for architectural efficiency (PLE, parameter sharing). E4B ≈ performs like 4B dense.
explanation: Specific to Gemma 3n/4 series. E4B has 8B total params but 4.5B 'effective' due to Per-Layer Embeddings inflating count. Distinguishes from raw param count.

name: K / M (Context length)
oneliner: 128K = 128,000 token context. 1M = 1 million. Distinguishes long-context variants from defaults.
explanation: Not always in the name — sometimes only in the model card. When present, distinguishes extended-context variant. Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-1M, Llama 3.1 (128K).

name: x (MoE expert notation)
oneliner: Mixtral-style: 8x7B = 8 experts of ~7B each. Total ≠ 8×7B due to shared attention layers. Active = top-k experts worth.
explanation: Popularized by Mixtral. 8x7B total ≈ 46.7B (not 56B) because attention params are shared. Active per token ≈ 12.9B (top-2 of 8).

name: -base / -raw / -pretrain / -pt
oneliner: No instruction tuning. Next-token prediction only. The starting point for fine-tuning. Google uses -pt, others use -base or no suffix.
explanation: Some repos omit suffix for base (Llama 3.1 has no suffix for base, adds -Instruct for tuned). Google Gemma uses -pt. Functionally: generates completions, not conversations.

resources.label: HuggingFace model hub (base vs instruct naming)

name: -it / -instruct / -chat
oneliner: -it (Google Gemma), -Instruct (Meta Llama, Mistral), -Chat (Qwen 1/2). Same concept, different branding. Designed for instruction-following.
explanation: All mean: SFT + alignment applied. Qwen shifted from -Chat to -Instruct in Qwen2.5. The distinction between -instruct and -chat is branding, not technical.

name: -sft / -dpo / -rlhf / -orpo / -kto
oneliner: Signals which training stage/method produced the model. -sft = supervised only. -dpo = DPO-aligned. Often released in pairs for comparison.
explanation: Useful for researchers comparing alignment methods. Example: zephyr-7b-sft-full (SFT only) vs zephyr-7b-beta (SFT+DPO).

name: -hf
oneliner: Weights converted to HuggingFace transformers format (safetensors + config.json). Does NOT mean 'hosted on HF'. Without -hf may need custom loading.
explanation: Critical for usability. Llama-2-7b-hf (converted) vs Llama-2-7b (original Megatron format). Modern releases (Llama 3+) are HF-native, making suffix less necessary.

name: -Coder / -Code
oneliner: CPT or fine-tuned on code corpora. Distinct models, not just prompting. Qwen2.5-Coder, DeepSeek-Coder, CodeLlama.
explanation: Some prefix (CodeLlama), some suffix (Qwen2.5-Coder). Usually via continued pre-training on hundreds of billions of code tokens.

name: -V / -VL / -Vision
oneliner: Accepts image (and sometimes video) inputs. -VL (Qwen), -Vision (Llama). Has an added vision encoder (often ViT) + projection layers.
explanation: Qwen2.5-VL-7B, Llama-3.2-11B-Vision. These are architecturally different from text-only — contain a vision encoder.

name: -Math
oneliner: Specialized for mathematical reasoning via training data and reward signals. Qwen2.5-Math, DeepSeek-Math.
explanation: Evaluated on GSM8K, MATH, competition benchmarks. Some use tool-augmented reasoning (code interpreter for computation).

name: -Embed / -Embedding
oneliner: Produces fixed-size vectors for retrieval/similarity, not generative text. Often encoder-only or uses mean pooling over decoder.
explanation: bge-large, gte-Qwen2-7B, nomic-embed-text. Output: single vector per input. Used for RAG, search, similarity.

name: -Guard / -Shield
oneliner: Detects harmful content, prompt injections, policy violations. Outputs safe/unsafe classification, not generative text. Llama-Guard-3.
explanation: Llama Guard (Inan et al., 2023) pioneered the pattern. Used as a filter alongside generative models.

foundational_papers.title: Llama Guard: LLM-based Input-Output Safeguard for Human-AI Conversations

name: -MRL
oneliner: Embedding model supporting variable-dimensionality outputs — truncate to smaller sizes with minimal quality loss. Named after Russian nesting dolls.
explanation: nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (MRL-capable). Allows storage/compute trade-offs at inference. Train once, serve at any dimension.

name: -Reward / -RM
oneliner: Outputs scalar preference scores for (prompt, response) pairs. Used in RLHF pipelines or best-of-N reranking. Not generative.
explanation: Starling-RM-34B, tulu-v3.1-8b-rl-rm. Critical RLHF component. Takes (prompt, response) → scalar score.

name: -Nano/-Micro/-Mini/-Small/-Tiny/-Lite/-Pro/-Turbo/-Large/-Max/-Ultra
expansion: Relative scale/quality tiers within a model family
oneliner: NOT standardized across families. Mistral 'Small' = 24B. Phi 'Mini' = 3.8B. Always check actual param count. -Turbo = speed-optimized (OpenAI convention).
explanation: These are relative to each family's range, not absolute sizes. -Lite usually implies deliberate distillation. -Turbo implies speed optimization (distillation or arch changes). -Pro/-Max/-Ultra more common in API naming than HuggingFace repos.

name: Scout / Maverick (Llama 4)
oneliner: Scout: 17B active, 16 experts (109B total), 10M context. Maverick: 17B active, 128 experts (400B+ total), higher quality.
explanation: Released April 2025. Same active param count but different expert counts. 16E/128E in the name = number of experts.

name: -MOE / -MoE
oneliner: Explicit MoE tag. Qwen2.5-MoE-A14B, DBRX (MoE). Mixtral uses 8x7B notation instead.
explanation: Some families use -MoE suffix, others encode it in the expert notation (8x7B). Check for num_local_experts in config.

name: -Long
oneliner: Trained or fine-tuned for longer sequences. Some use -Long suffix, others embed context length in name (128K, 1M).
explanation: Methods: RoPE scaling (YaRN, NTK, ABF), continued training on long documents. gradientai/Llama-3-8B-Instruct-Gradient-1048k.

name: -merged / -unmerged
oneliner: -merged: LoRA folded into base weights (standalone, no overhead, irreversible). -unmerged: adapter separate (can hot-swap, needs PEFT loading).
explanation: Merged = larger files (full model) but simpler deployment. Unmerged = small adapter files + base model, flexible but needs PEFT-aware code.

name: -ft / -full-ft / -fft / -lora / -qlora / -adapter
oneliner: -ft: generic fine-tuned. -full-ft/-fft: all params updated. -lora/-qlora: adapter type. -adapter: generic (usually bottleneck). Check model card for details.
explanation: -ft is ambiguous (could be any method). -lora adapter files are typically 10-100MB. -full-ft produces full model copies.
